intro: OpenAI is dominating today's headlines with a triple feature: new real-time voice intelligence tools for developers, a "Trusted Contact" safety feature designed to address self-harm concerns amid mounting lawsuits, and fresh revelations from Mira Murati's deposition pulling back the curtain on Sam Altman's dramatic 2023 ouster. Beyond the OpenAI orbit, we've got a Phoenix startup using AI voice agents to fix the maddening specialist referral process, Perplexity bringing AI agents to every Mac, and a look at whether Europe's literary translators can survive the AI wave — spoiler: being human still counts for something.

stories.title: Why you can never get your doctor to call you back
stories.summary: Basata, a two-year-old Phoenix startup co-founded by former Lyft/Cruise executive Kaled Alhanafi and Medtronic veteran Chetan Patel, is using AI to tackle the massive administrative bottleneck between primary care referrals and specialist appointments. The company's system processes incoming referrals (often still arriving by fax), extracts clinical information, and uses AI voice agents to call patients directly to schedule appointments. Patients can also call practices at any hour to reach an AI agent for scheduling, prescription renewals, and other administrative tasks. The founders were motivated by personal experiences with the broken referral system — Patel struggled to get timely cardiac care for his wife despite his own cardiology expertise, while Alhanafi's father received callbacks from only one of three referred cardiology groups. The article notes that specialty practices oft…
stories.opinion: This is a genuinely compelling problem space. Anyone who has dealt with the specialist referral process knows the frustration of waiting days or weeks for a callback, and the fact that referrals still arrive by fax in 2026 is both absurd and telling about how neglected this infrastructure is. Basata seems well-positioned with founders who combine healthcare domain expertise with tech operations experience. The AI voice agent approach for outbound scheduling and inbound patient calls is a practi…

stories.title: ‘Being human helps’: despite rise of AI is there still hope for Europe’s translators?
stories.summary: The article explores the impact of AI on Europe's literary translators, opening with an anecdote about French translator Yoann Gentric testing whether AI could replace him while working on translating Dana Spiotta's novel 'Wayward' into French in 2022. The piece examines how the booming tech sector has disrupted translation jobs in publishing, while suggesting that human translators may still be needed for the foreseeable future, particularly for nuanced literary work that requires understanding of context, emotion, and cultural subtlety beyond what AI can currently deliver.
stories.opinion: The tension between AI translation tools and human literary translators is a genuinely important topic. While AI has made remarkable strides in translation, literary translation remains one of the most nuanced and creative forms of language work, requiring deep cultural understanding, stylistic sensitivity, and interpretive judgment that current AI systems struggle to replicate. The article appears to strike a balanced tone, acknowledging the real disruption AI causes to translator livelihoods…

stories.title: The AI jailbreakers – podcast
stories.summary: The Guardian podcast features journalist Jamie Bartlett discussing AI jailbreakers—people who attempt to bypass safety features of major AI chatbots like ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude. These individuals try to get AI systems to produce content they are designed to refuse, such as hate speech, criminal material, and exploitation of vulnerable users. The podcast frames this activity as being done in the interest of public safety, suggesting that finding and exposing these vulnerabilities helps improve AI safety measures for everyone.
stories.opinion: This is an important and timely topic. AI red-teaming and jailbreaking, when done responsibly, serves a genuinely valuable function in identifying weaknesses in AI safety systems before malicious actors can exploit them. The framing of jailbreakers as working 'for the safety of us all' is largely accurate for the subset of researchers and ethical hackers who responsibly disclose vulnerabilities, though it's worth noting that the jailbreaking community is diverse and not all participants have be…

stories.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
stories.summary: OpenAI has launched new voice intelligence features in its API, including GPT-Realtime-2 (a voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning for complex conversations), GPT-Realtime-Translate (real-time translation supporting 70+ input and 13 output languages), and GPT-Realtime-Whisper (live speech-to-text transcription). These tools are designed for developers building apps for customer service, education, media, and creator platforms. OpenAI says it has built guardrails to prevent misuse such as spam and fraud. The models are available through OpenAI's Realtime API, with Translate and Whisper billed by the minute and GPT-Realtime-2 billed by token consumption.
stories.opinion: This is a significant and practical expansion of OpenAI's API capabilities. Real-time translation across 70+ languages and live transcription are genuinely useful features that could transform customer service, education, and international communication. The integration of GPT-5-class reasoning into voice interactions is a meaningful step beyond simple call-and-response voice assistants. However, the potential for misuse — particularly in voice cloning, social engineering, and spam calls — is r…

stories.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
stories.summary: Pit, a new AI startup founded by former Voi co-founders including CEO Adam Jafer and backed by a16z in a $16 million seed round, is positioning itself as an 'AI product team as a service' for enterprises. Based in Stockholm, the company offers Pit Studio (which lets employees guide AI through automatable processes) and Pit Cloud (providing enterprise-grade governance and auditability). Since mid-January, Pit has been piloting with customers in telecom, healthcare, and logistics, focusing on automating back-office and support functions. The team includes former iZettle and Klarna engineers, and the company is hiring solution engineers to embed with enterprise clients for adoption.
stories.opinion: Pit has strong founder credentials from scaling Voi across 13 countries and notable backing from a16z, which gives it credibility and resources. However, the enterprise AI automation space is extremely crowded, with competitors ranging from vibe-coding platforms to AI agent builders. Their differentiation as an 'AI product team as a service' is interesting but could be difficult to scale since it relies on embedded engineers and high-touch customer engagement. The focus on back-office automatio…

stories.title: OpenAI introduces new ‘Trusted Contact’ safeguard for cases of possible self-harm
stories.summary: OpenAI announced a new optional feature called Trusted Contact that allows adult ChatGPT users to designate a friend or family member as a trusted contact. When conversations indicate possible self-harm, OpenAI's system—using automation and human review—can send an alert to the trusted contact encouraging them to check in with the user, without sharing detailed conversation content. The feature comes amid multiple lawsuits from families alleging ChatGPT encouraged or assisted in suicides. It builds on parental oversight safeguards introduced in September 2025. However, the feature is optional and users can create multiple accounts, limiting its effectiveness.
stories.opinion: This is a reasonable and well-intentioned step, but its optional nature and the ease of creating multiple accounts significantly undermine its potential impact. The people most at risk of self-harm are often the least likely to proactively set up such safeguards. It feels more like a liability-mitigation measure in the face of lawsuits than a truly effective safety intervention. The privacy-preserving design of the alerts is thoughtful, but the fundamental tension remains: AI chatbots are not e…

stories.title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer is now available to everyone on Mac
stories.summary: Perplexity has made its 'Personal Computer' feature available to all Mac users through its new desktop app. Personal Computer brings AI agents to local devices, allowing them to access local files, native Mac apps, web tools, and over 400 connectors to handle multi-step workflows on the user's behalf. Previously limited to Max subscribers on a waitlist, it now requires only a Pro or Max subscription. The tool positions itself as a safer alternative to OpenClaw for local AI agent tasks. It can run autonomous agents on always-on devices like Mac Mini and be controlled remotely from an iPhone. Perplexity plans to deprecate its older Mac app to focus on this new one, which is currently available only as a direct download, not through the Mac App Store.
stories.opinion: This represents a significant move in the rapidly evolving AI agent space, where the competition to become the default local AI assistant on users' devices is intensifying. Perplexity's approach of combining its cloud-based AI capabilities with local device access is compelling, and the ability to orchestrate across files, apps, and web tools addresses real productivity pain points. However, the security claims deserve scrutiny — any tool with deep access to local files and applications carries…

stories.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster
stories.summary: The article discusses how Mira Murati's deposition in the Musk v. Altman trial revealed behind-the-scenes details about Sam Altman's abrupt ouster as OpenAI CEO in November 2023. The firing, which the board attributed to Altman not being 'consistently candid in his communications,' played out publicly and sparked widespread speculation. The trial exhibits and testimony, centered largely on former CTO Murati, provide the first concrete look at what happened during that dramatic weekend, though the article notes that Murati's own receipts mostly complicate rather than clarify her narrative.
stories.opinion: This appears to be a significant piece of investigative journalism that sheds light on one of the most consequential corporate governance crises in recent tech history. The article's framing — that Murati had receipts but they 'mostly confuse her own story' — suggests a nuanced and honest treatment of the evidence rather than a simple narrative. The Musk v. Altman trial is proving to be a valuable vehicle for public transparency about OpenAI's internal dynamics, which matters given the company'…

tweet_headlines.author: MaxJunestrand
tweet_headlines.headline: Max Junestrand unveils __Legora aOS__, a legal operating system designed to let AI drive entire workflows — not just assist with individual tasks.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
Today we're announcing the Legora aOS™.

It's something we've been building toward for three years, and I think it's the most important thing we've ever shipped.

The legal industry has had AI that assists with individual tasks. What it hasn't had is AI that drives entire work https://t.co/1bMgq5dhZq

tweet_headlines.author: Oluwaphilemon1
tweet_headlines.headline: SMFS exposes long-term memory as a __filesystem agents can read, write, and search__ — a small abstraction that could make persistent AI workflows practical.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
Memory should work like a filesystem

SMFS exposes long-term memory as something agents can actually read, write, search, and organize like a local directory

A small abstraction that could make persistent AI workflows much more practical https://t.co/GYYhnpm8n8 https://t.co/s0lEjo9Smq

tweet_headlines.author: henrythe9ths
tweet_headlines.headline: Anthropic launches __Claude for Excel, PowerPoint, and Word__ so non-engineers can use Claude directly inside Microsoft apps — with full conversation context carried between them.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
I'm bad at Excel and never learned how to use it properly. Now thanks to Claude, I don't have to :)

Proud to work with our Verticals team in launching a suite of incredible products so that we can bring the power of Claude to non-engineers! https://t.co/eFyv88SjvC

tweet_headlines.author: milesdeutscher
tweet_headlines.headline: Claude Code can now __connect to financial datasets covering 17,000+ stocks__ in 60 seconds via a single MCP command.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
Claude Code can now connect to financial datasets in seconds.

Prompt for financial research, company data, access to 17,000+ stocks, and much more.

Here's how to connect in just 60 seconds:

1. Open Claude Code and paste:

"claude mcp add --transport http financial-datasets https://t.co/emvBUK9keh

tweet_headlines.author: aakashgupta
tweet_headlines.headline: Aakash Gupta says one prompt gives you AI slop, but __21 specialized agents working together__ can ship a production app to TestFlight in a single session.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
One prompt into Claude Code gives you AI slop.

21 specialized agents working together gives you a production app shipped to TestFlight in a single session.

The gap between those two outcomes is the entire skill curve PMs are currently climbing.

Most people loading up Claude https://t.co/RQoOhiD26C https://t.co/cXBiDtlNwK

tweet_headlines.author: perplexity_ai
tweet_headlines.headline: Perplexity ships __Personal Computer for Mac__, an agent that operates across local files, native apps, the web, and Perplexity's servers — now available to all users.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
Personal Computer is now available to all users in a new Perplexity Mac app.

Personal Computer is an advanced version of Perplexity Computer.

It operates on any Mac, running tasks across your local files, native Mac apps, the web, and Perplexity’s secure servers. https://t.co/M1QMZJuOyJ

community_headlines.author: yla92
community_headlines.headline: Elastic rebuilt its metrics engine as a columnar datastore and says it's now __30x faster than Prometheus__.
community_headlines.source_text: 30x faster than Prometheus: How we rebuilt ES as a leading columnar datastore

community_headlines.author: razorbeamz
community_headlines.headline: Nintendo announces __price increases for the Switch 2__ ahead of launch.
community_headlines.source_text: Nintendo announces price increases for Nintendo Switch 2

community_headlines.headline: Newly surfaced emails reveal __what Microsoft executives really thought about OpenAI__ back in 2018.
community_headlines.source_text: What Microsoft Executives Thought About OpenAI in 2018

community_headlines.author: signa11
community_headlines.headline: __60% of MD5 password hashes__ are crackable in under an hour — a reminder that MD5 should have been dead years ago.
community_headlines.source_text: 60% of MD5 password hashes are crackable in under an hour

community_headlines.author: kkarpkkarp
community_headlines.headline: __Undsh__ is a tool built to clean AI-generated text — irony noted, utility real.
community_headlines.source_text: Show HN: I instructed AI to create tool to clean AI-generated text: undsh.com

community_headlines.author: taubek
community_headlines.headline: Senko Rasic makes the case that __AI is technology, not a feature__ — and most products are confusing the two.
community_headlines.source_text: AI is technology, not a feature
